entries.definition:
q–f–w back of the neck, nape, back of the head, the reverse; to
follow, to track, to send after; to rhyme, poem; to slander,
slander; advantage, hospitality. Of this root, two forms occur five
times in the Qur’an: تَقْفُ taqfū once and قَفَّيْنَا qaffaynā four times.

تَقْفُ taqfū [imperf. of v. قفا qafā, trans.] to pursue, to follow, to
track, to seek (17:36) وَلَا تَقْفُ مَا لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ and do not pry into
that which you are not supposed to know [lit. and do not seek that
of which you have no knowledge].

قَفَّى qaffā [v. II, intrans.] to cause to follow, to cause to come
after (5:46) وَقَفَّيْنَا عَلَىٰ آثَارِهِمْ بِعِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ مُصَدِّقًا لِمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ مِنَ التَّوْرَاةِ
We caused Jesus, son of Mary, to follow in their footsteps,
confirming the Torah [that had come] before him.
